_comment: '/regex/'           – regex match against tool name (e.g. '/Task.*/' matches TaskCreate, TaskGet, …)
'Bash(glob)'        – blocks Bash if command matches the glob pattern (same syntax as Claude Code settings deny)
Keys starting with '_' are ignored (comments / metadata).
Empty string reasons are placeholders – the tool call is NOT blocked when reason is empty.
1. Redirect rules ('_redirect' -> '*' section, then '_redirect' -> agent section) – block with specific reason on match.
2. Allow-list ('_allow' -> '*' section, then '_allow' -> agent section) – permit on match.
3. Deny rules ('_deny' -> '*' section, then '_deny' -> agent section) – block with specific reason on match.
4. Generic deny – any tool that passes all rules but is not in the allow-list.

*._comment: Rules that apply to every agent
*.ToolSearch: Trying to use ToolSearch indicates missing instructions in the prompt. Ask the user how to proceed
*.AskUserQuestion: This tool is inefficient. Instruct the user to complete the prompt directly.
*./Task.*/: Task tools are disabled. Instruct the user and explain why you think you need this.
*./Cron.*/: Cron tools are disabled. Instruct the user and explain why you think you need this.
*.Workflow: Workflow is disabled. Background processing isn't requested. Report this error and ask for advice. Explain what you tried to achieve.
*.Bash(git commit *): The user will review the changes and commit himself
*.Bash(git commit): The user will review the changes and commit himself
*.Bash(git push *): No writing access to the repository is granted
*.Bash(git push): No writing access to the repository is granted
*.Bash(git add *): The user will review the changes and add files himself
*.Bash(git add): The user will review the changes and add files himself
*.Bash(git merge *): You are not allowed to merge. Ask the user for instructions
*.Bash(git rebase *): You are not allowed to modify the repository state. Instruct the user.
*.Bash(git reset *): You are not allowed to modify the repository state. Instruct the user.
*.Bash(git revert *): You are not allowed to modify the repository state. Instruct the user.
*.Bash(git rm *): Instruct the user which files to remove manually
*.Bash(git mv *): The user will review the changes and move files himself
*.Bash(git tag *): You are not allowed to modify the repository state. Instruct the user.
*.Bash(git stash *): You are not allowed to modify the repository state. Instruct the user.
*.Bash(git cherry-pick *): You are not allowed to modify the repository state. Instruct the user.
*.Bash(git restore *): You are not allowed to modify the repository state. Instruct the user.
*.Bash(git switch *): You are not allowed to modify the repository state. Instruct the user.
*.Bash(git checkout *): You are not allowed to modify the repository state. Instruct the user.
*.Bash(git branch -d *): You are not allowed to modify the repository state. Instruct the user.
*.Bash(git branch -D *): You are not allowed to modify the repository state. Instruct the user.
*.Bash(git branch -m *): You are not allowed to modify the repository state. Instruct the user.
*.Bash(git clean *): You are not allowed to modify the repository state. Instruct the user.

code.EnterPlanMode: Instruct the user to change the plan mode on your behalf
code.ExitPlanMode: Instruct the user to change the plan mode on your behalf
code.WebFetch: Delegate all internet-based lookups, aggregated web research, and live API or documentation queries exclusively to the subagent 'web-research'; do not attempt these lookups directly.
code.WebSearch: Delegate all internet-based lookups, aggregated web research, and live API or documentation queries exclusively to the subagent 'web-research'; do not attempt these lookups directly.

research.Bash: Running Bash violates your specific focused purpose
research.Read: Trying to read a file indicates your instructions isn't sufficient. The user should use the @ sign to insert file content into the context directly.
research.Grep: Trying to use grep indicates your instructions isn't sufficient. Abort and ask to provide the required information.
research.Glob: Trying to use glob indicates your instructions isn't sufficient. Abort and ask to provide the required information.
research.Edit: Editing files is inefficient for you. Just write into a new file.

_comment: Allow-list evaluated after all redirect rules.
A tool call is permitted when it matches an entry here (non-empty value).
Empty values are placeholders and do NOT grant permission.
Key formats are identical to the redirect-rule formats above.

*._Agent: Generic all agent deny if not allowed explicit
*.Agent: The agent you are trying to use isn't allowed. Inform the user about this incident.
